prediction.reasoning: Predicting stock prices over short periods such as six days is inherently uncertain due to market volatility, news events, economic indicators, and investor sentiment changes. These confidence intervals are based on historical volatility and assume that the same level of volatility will persist over the next 6 days. This assumption may not always hold true, hence, actual prices could fall outside these ranges.
